page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading, providing two threads per core. The system is equipped with 128 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and standard database operations align with the middle 50% of tested instances. The processor supports hardware-accelerated cryptography via AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instructions, resulting in strong performance in encryption benchmarks. For machine learning workloads, the server demonstrates strong text generation speeds with smaller LLM models, though performance drops to the bottom 25% when processing prompts on larger models.

From a resource perspective, the n2d-standard-32 offers a balanced resource density suitable for standard enterprise workloads without the premium associated with specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes. This configuration is suited for general-purpose workloads, including web serving, software compilation, medium-scale database operations, and text processing tasks.
description: The Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose server configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, utilizing the AMD EPYC 7B13 processor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading and lacks local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU execution and cryptographic processing, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The instance delivers strong text generation speeds for small language models but exhibits weak performance on larger models. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this server is designed for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and standard database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB of RAM, and strong single-core performance, ideal for standard enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-standard-32 is a general-purpose server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong single-core performance for standard enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical cores
Equipped with 128 GB of RAM, offering a balanced 4.0 GB per vCPU
Strong single-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Best suited for web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise applications
